shared_properties: The overlay covers the full 1920x1080 viewport to support the Tab-triggered light-path preview.
Core palette metadata remains primary #C9A84C, background #0D0B14, and accent #D4682A while preview colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
Transparency holes preserve world readability so the overlay adds guidance without erasing puzzle geometry.
differences: This state sheet contains beam-only, shadow-only, and combined-preview variants.
Hazard beams use semi-transparent exposure white, safe-shadow areas use translucent teal, and combined mode preserves both with gold guide arrows.
A thin corner hint plate in the upper-right area reserves space for the preview toggle label.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the Tab light-preview overlay, beam analysis, and safe-shadow visualization rule.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the soft transparent overlay treatment that fits the tower mood without flattening the scene.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the need to preserve player silhouette readability beneath the overlay.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle or fullscreen ImageBundle on the overlay camera.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the overlay atlas during FloorLoading and toggle the appropriate frame when preview mode changes.
runtime_notes: Render the overlay on the HUD camera with transparency preserved.
Swap states instantly on Tab input without rebuilding the widget tree.
Keep blend strength deterministic through authored alpha rather than post-process grading.

context.usage: Optional full-screen overlay that previews current beam routes and safe-shadow coverage when analysis mode is toggled.

preview_notes: Upper-right hint plate width is 180 px.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 5760x1080 px with three 1920x1080 frames and 0 px spacing.
Beam-only, shadow-only, and combined states remain visually distinct while preserving puzzle-legible transparency.
The overlay does not obscure scale readouts, Umbra silhouette, or exit-door framing at common camera positions.
